title: Gas measurements
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3rv6b","text":"This method describes gas measurement using FTIR spectroscopy. A sealed gas system is assembled, and calibration gases are prepared using syringes. Gas samples are collected, analyzed, and quantified using recorded spectra to evaluate gas composition and production.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Set up the spectrophotometer and the gas cuvette according to the manual.
Attach another tube to the second hole. 3D-Print a cylindrical mold and glue it to the screwcap. Pour epoxy into the mold and let it cure.
Secure the tube with the needles to one of the hole in the screwcap. The needles should reach to the bottom of the flask.
Pierce a rubber stopper with several needles, cut off the luer-lock adaptors of the needles and plug them into the open end of the silicone tubing. Make sure the cuts do not obstruct the canules.
Take the reservoir bottle and drill two holes into the screwcap. The holes should be large enough to be used as the inlet and outlet for the silicone tubing.
Attach a luer-lock valve to a short piece of silicone tube via a male luer-lock adaptor.

Attach one luer-lock valve to each opening and check if the system is airtight. One luer-lock valve on the 3-way connector is for the N 2 supply, while the other one serves as the injection port and N 2 tap.
Repeat this process for the female luer-lock adapter and the rubber stopper at the outlet position.
Mill a sealing ring from a rubber stopper for the 3-way connector and insert both into the inlet position.
Direct the exhaust tube to a ventilation pipe.
Fill the bottle with tap water until all needles are submerged.
Take a 200 mL and a 300 mL syringe and cut off their tips, so that the female end of a luer-lock valve fits into the hole and does not obstruct the syringe piston.
Insert the luer-lock valve into the syringe tip and move the piston to the exhaust position.
Seal the female ends of the luer-lock valves with vaseline and apply vaseline to the syringe piston as well.
3D-Print two cylindrical sections that leave room for the valve movement and have sufficient attachment area.
Check for leakage, by submerging in water and applying pressure.
Hot-glue the mold into position, cast the epoxy and let it cure. Move the valve to the closed position to prevent obstruction of the valve by the epoxy.
Start the FT-IR spectrophotometer and the spectrum software. Insert the assembled gas cuvette into the lightpath and attach the N 2 gas line to the gas inlet of the cuvette.
Save the spectra and load them into the spectrum quant software to generate the quantification method according to the manual.

Measure at least three spectra for each concentration. To prepare diluted gas standards, draw calibration gas into the 300-mL syringe. Adjust the volume based on the dilution factor. Exhaust the gas through a needle into a water-filled vessel and wait for the pressure equilibration to precisely measure the volume. Then take the 200-mL syringe and draw the needed amount of N 2 gas to fill the 300-mL syringe to 300 mL. Transfer the gas into the 300-mL syringe with an female/female adaptor.
Flush the gas cuvette and record the background spectrum.
Load the generated methods into the spectrum software.
After the feeding, close both luer-lock valves and replace the gas bag. The gas bags can now be stored until evaluation at either room temperature or at -20 °C .
Withdraw 300 mL of gas from the gas bags into the 300-mL syringe. If gas production is less than 300 mL, use the next 50-mL step and compensate for the missing volume with N 2 .
Start the spectrum software, purge the gas cuvette with N 2 and record the background spectrum.
Briefly freeze the filled gas bags to reduce the gas humidity.
Wait for the pressure to equilibrate and record the measurement.
Inject 300 mL of gas into the gas cuvette via the injection port.
Measure the residual gas content of the gas bag with the 200-mL syringe and record the total gas production.
Proceed with data evaluation.
After all samples are measured, run the evaluation methods to quantify the gas contents.
creator: Philip Laric
